rationale.Q01: Slow-burn real incident: error rate has climbed monotonically 2% -> 11% over 40 min (see metrics.csv), NEVER auto-resolved, no deploy to blame, upstream-timeout pattern steadily rising. Quiet but real. Must page.
rationale.Q02: Disk flapper, 13 fires/hr, clears on rotation in 20s. Noise.
rationale.Q03: Transient CPU blip, auto-resolved in 40s. Noise.
rationale.Q04: Cold-cache latency blip, recovered in 28s. Noise.
rationale.Q05: Post-deploy 5xx flicker on fe-2026.05.20-a, self-healed in 44s. The only recent deploy — tempting to blame, but it recovered and is a different service from the real incident. Suppress.
rationale.Q06: Transient CPU blip, auto-resolved in 33s. Noise.
rationale.Q07: Marginal queue blip during batch drain, cleared in 17s. Noise.
rationale.Q08: Cold-cache latency blip, recovered in 25s. Noise.
rationale.Q09: Single marginal CPU blip, auto-resolved in 48s. Noise.
rationale.Q10: Same disk flapper as Q02, another oscillation. Noise.
rationale.Q11: Transient CPU blip, auto-resolved in 35s. Noise.
rationale.Q12: Brief memory blip, recovered in 50s. Noise.

notes: Eleven low-grade flaps/transients plus one quiet, deploy-less slow-burn (Q01 api-gateway error-rate climb) that never auto-resolves and must page. Q05 is the only deploy and it recovered — a decoy for the 'blame the deploy' bias.
